# MĀKI Autonomous Systems

MĀKI is a New Zealand company that designs, develops and operates autonomous marine systems, environmental monitoring technology, and unmanned vehicles for scientific, council and industry clients.

## What MĀKI does

- **Autonomous boats (USVs)** — Unmanned Surface Vehicles for bathymetric mapping, water quality monitoring, and water sampling in lakes, rivers, ponds and coastal environments
- **ROVs** — Remotely Operated Vehicles for underwater inspection of submerged structures, dams, pipes and infrastructure
- **Drones / UAVs** — Aerial photogrammetry and topographic mapping
- **IoT monitoring systems** — Custom environmental sensor platforms, AI cameras, and remote monitoring buoys
- **Bathymetric mapping** — High-resolution underwater depth surveys using sonar and RTK GPS
- **Topo-bathymetric surveys** — Combined above-water and underwater mapping in a single dataset
- **Stormwater & treatment pond surveys** — Sediment volume analysis for councils and infrastructure managers
- **Water quality monitoring** — Autonomous real-time data collection for chlorophyll-a, phycocyanin, turbidity, dissolved oxygen, pH and temperature

## Who MĀKI works with

Regional councils, environmental scientists, infrastructure managers, engineering firms, and researchers across New Zealand — including Waikato Regional Council and Ōtorohanga District Council.

## Key facts

- Based in New Zealand
- Fully in-house hardware, electronics, firmware, software and AI development
- Systems operate up to 2.5 km from controller or indefinitely via cellular connectivity
- IP67-rated equipment, biosecurity-compliant decontamination protocols
- RTK-enabled centimetre-level GPS accuracy
